title: Linaria simplex
taxonomy: Linaria simplex is classified as a species within the genus Linaria, which belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. This genus is a group of flowering plants commonly known as toadflax or toad's eye. The family Plantaginaceae is a part of the larger order Lamiales, which includes plants with bilaterally symmetrical flowers. The order Lamiales is a subgroup of the larger clade Asterids, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Campanulaceae and Lamiaceae. Linaria simplex is further classified as a member of the section Linaria sect. Linaria.

intro: Linaria simplex, also known as Toadflax, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Linaria simplex include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Linaria simplex include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory afflictions.
